title: OMNIA-MINIMAL: Structural Stability Beyond Surface Correctness
description: OMNIA-MINIMAL is a minimal reproducible demonstration of the OMNIA ecosystem. It demonstrates one narrow claim: correctness is not structural stability. The repository shows that an output can pass surface correctness while failing structural stability under independent transformations. OMNIA-MINIMAL does not infer meaning, decide truth, or replace human judgment. It emits bounded post-hoc structural signals: STABLE, FRAGILE, and DIVERGENT.
notes: Boundary: measurement != inference != decision. A stable signal is not automatically true. A fragile signal is not automatically false. The output is a structural signal, not a verdict.
